operating_state: Total revenues increased 7% to $74.6 billion driven by Google Search growth and Google Cloud acceleration of 28% achieving operating profitability versus prior year loss. YouTube ads remained flat while Google Network declined, with paid clicks increasing 8% offset by cost-per-click decline from device mix shifts. Google Services operating income increased substantially while Other Bets losses decreased from reduced valuation-based compensation liabilities.
cost_structure: Cost of revenues increased to $31.9 billion maintaining 43% of total revenues, driven by higher YouTube content acquisition and hardware costs partially offset by depreciation reduction from extending server useful lives to six years. Operating expenses rose 4% with headcount growth and compensation increases. Workforce reduction charges totaled $2.0 billion with office space optimization generating additional charges.
material_events: Completed $70 billion share repurchase authorization with new $70 billion program approved, repurchasing $30.2 billion during six months. Consolidated Google Research Brain team and DeepMind to form Google DeepMind, accelerating AI progress and reported within unallocated corporate costs beginning Q2. European Commission reduced 2018 fine with appeal filed.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency headwinds reduced reported growth with constant currency revenue growth of 9% exceeding reported 7% due to dollar strengthening against major currencies. IRS announced temporary relief allowing pre-2022 foreign tax credit regulations for fiscal years 2022-2023. Effective tax rate increased to 16.1% from changes in tax return positions.
forward_outlook: Revenue backlog reached $60.6 billion primarily from Google Cloud commitments with approximately half expected within 24 months. Capital expenditures decreased with continued technical infrastructure investment planned through 2024 supporting AI initiatives. Management expects most remaining workforce reduction employees to exit by end of 2023 with ongoing cost optimization beyond completed reductions.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple antitrust proceedings including DOJ lawsuit scheduled for September 2023 trial and EC Statement of Objections regarding advertising technology requiring response. European Commission fines totaling $9.5 billion remain under appeal with bank guarantees provided. Gross unrecognized tax benefits increased with purchase commitments creating timing uncertainty.
